title: Syracuse asks court to replace current redeveloper of Skyline Apartments with receiver
summary: Syracuse is petitioning NY State Supreme Court to replace Clear Investment Group with a court-appointed receiver for the Skyline Apartments on James Street. The 352-unit building has deteriorated despite Clear's 2024 promises of $4.5M in upgrades; the city seeks $355K in fines, $2M in escrow for repairs, and code violation corrections. This action addresses a critical housing shortage amid documented property failures including fire, flooding, and squatter occupation.
department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Mayor and city leadership applying judicial pressure on private developer for non-performance; framing as urgent due to housing shortage and public safety failures. Escalation from administrative oversight to court intervention.
